strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Georgia (UGA) is the state's flagship public land-grant and sea-grant research university. It is known for its rigorous academic programs, vibrant college town environment in Athens, and highly selective admissions process, particularly for out-of-state students [cite: 1, 9].

analyst_takeaways.insight: Overall acceptance rates have dropped to 33%, with a sharp divide between in-state (~50%) and out-of-state (~28%) acceptance rates, driven by a mandate to keep the incoming class at 80% Georgia residents [cite: 3, 12].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Out-of-state applicants face a significantly higher barrier to entry and must present exceptional academic profiles to be competitive.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Course rigor is the most critical factor in UGA's review process. The average admitted student has taken 10 or more AP, IB, or Dual Enrollment courses and boasts a recalculated core GPA over 4.15 [cite: 7, 13].
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students avoiding challenging coursework to protect their GPA are at a distinct disadvantage compared to those taking the most rigorous courses available at their high schools [cite: 12].

analyst_takeaways.insight: UGA offers a classic college town environment with heavily integrated Greek life, over 800 student organizations, and major SEC D-I athletics [cite: 1, 10].
analyst_takeaways.implication: The campus appeals strongly to students seeking a traditional, spirited, and highly active collegiate experience.

analyst_takeaways.insight: UGA provides excellent value, especially for in-state students eligible for the HOPE or Zell Miller scholarships, resulting in a low net cost of attendance relative to post-graduate earnings [cite: 8, 14].
analyst_takeaways.implication: It is an exceptionally high-ROI option for Georgia residents, though out-of-state students face much steeper tuition costs.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: UGA is situated in Athens, famously known as one of America's best college towns, featuring a vibrant music scene, prominent Greek life, and massive school spirit centered around SEC football [cite: 10, 12, 15].
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The main campus spans 762 acres with 465 buildings, making campus transit essential for navigating between classes, residence halls, and downtown Athens [cite: 9].
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution with over $500 million in annual research expenditures, UGA offers extensive undergraduate research opportunities, notably through the Center for Undergraduate Research Opportunities (CURO) and its active-learning initiatives [cite: 7, 14].

current_status.test_policy_details: Standardized test scores (SAT or ACT) are strictly required for admission per the University System of Georgia Board of Regents. UGA superscores both exams [cite: 4, 5, 16].

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data pulled from historical tracking; admitted numbers drop significantly based on yield [cite: 12, 17, 18].

gpa.notes: UGA recalculates GPA based exclusively on core academic courses (English, math, science, social science, foreign language) and adds 0.5 points for AP/IB courses if not already weighted by the high school [cite: 13, 21].

testing.policy_note: Submission rate is effectively 100% as UGA is not test-optional and strictly requires SAT or ACT scores [cite: 4, 13, 22].

colleges.name: Terry College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students should transfer into or declare as intended-business majors as early as possible. Admission to a Terry major is competitive and evaluated based on specific selection criteria, not guaranteed merely by meeting minimums [cite: 11, 23, 24].
colleges.student_archetype: Driven, career-oriented students targeting elite finance, accounting, or risk management roles.

majors.name: Finance
majors.special_requirements: Must have an overall GPA of 2.6+ and a UGA GPA of 2.0+, and complete the Terry Accounting Exam [cite: 23, 24, 25].
majors.notes: Finance is a high-demand major. Students not admitted to their first choice will be evaluated for their second choice within the college [cite: 24].

majors.name: Risk Management and Insurance
majors.special_requirements: Consistent with Terry College B.B.A. requirements [cite: 23, 25].
majors.notes: Consistently ranked #1 in the nation among all institutions [cite: 25, 26].

application_deadlines.notes: Materials due October 29. Decisions typically released in late November for in-state and mid-December for out-of-state [cite: 27, 28].
Materials due January 15. Decisions typically released in mid-March [cite: 28, 29].

supplemental_requirements.details: Short supplemental essays required as part of the holistic review [cite: 12, 28].

primary_factors: Rigor of high school course load
holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: UGA utilizes a 'whole-student review' model but is unabashedly academics-first. The rigor of high school coursework over four years is heavily scrutinized. UGA explicitly does NOT consider demonstrated interest or legacy status [cite: 4, 12, 28].

major_selection_tactics: UGA admits students to the university as a whole, not to specific majors. Intended major selection does not cap or restrict initial freshman admission [cite: 30, 31].
college_ranking_tactics: Applying Early Action (EA) signals strong preference and carries significantly higher acceptance rates, especially for in-state students (42% EA in-state vs. 23% EA out-of-state) [cite: 7, 22].
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Since UGA admits to the university rather than by major, an alternate major strategy is not highly relevant for first-year admissions. However, students intending to enter high-demand fields like Business must pass internal transfer requirements later [cite: 23, 31].

scholarships.name: Foundation Fellowship
scholarships.benefits: Effectively a full ride; includes stipends that approximate the annual cost of attendance plus full tuition waivers [cite: 32].

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Provides $500 per academic year for National Merit Finalists who select UGA as their first choice school [cite: 35].

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for 3s, 4s, and 5s depending on the specific exam.
exceptions: AP Calculus AB/BC requires a 3 or higher to exempt MATH 1113, though taking the math placement exam is still strongly recommended.
AP English Language/Literature requires a 3 or 4 for ENGL 1101 credit, and a 5 for ENGL 1102 credit.
ap_policy.usage: Satisfies core curriculum and degree requirements. UGA adds 0.5 to recalculated admissions GPA for AP classes [cite: 21, 36, 37].

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit awarded for Higher Level (HL) and Standard Level (SL) scores, generally requiring a 4+ for HL and 5+ for SL [cite: 37].

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Requires 30-59 completed hours with a 3.5+ transfer GPA, or 60+ hours with a 3.2+ transfer GPA for straightforward admission. Secondary review is triggered for lower GPAs [cite: 13, 23].

what_it_takes: Taking the most rigorous courses available at your high school (average admitted student has 10+ AP/IB/DE courses) [cite: 13].
Earning predominantly A's in core classes (UGA core GPA average is 4.19) [cite: 13].
essay_tips: Address any academic hardships or grade drops in the additional information section [cite: 15].
Highlight intellectual curiosity, community impact, and personal maturity [cite: 28].
red_flags: Avoiding challenging courses to protect GPA (UGA prefers a slightly lower GPA with maximum rigor) [cite: 12].
Applying out-of-state without exceptional statistics, given the 80% in-state mandate [cite: 7].
insights: UGA does not care about demonstrated interest or legacy status [cite: 4].
In-state students have a massive advantage; the university strictly manages yield to ensure an 80% resident class [cite: 3, 38].
